amiga:alienbreed2.rationale: Alien Breed 2 was designed first for AGA; that edition has more in-game colors, less-dithered loading screens, fuller sound effects, and voice absent from the reduced ECS release.

amiga:banshee.rationale: The AGA and CD32 releases use the same WHDLoad slave, but the current CD32 path has a reported controller failure while the AGA release works correctly.

amiga:chaosengine.rationale: The AGA release keeps the original gameplay while adding 256-color enhanced graphics; unlike CD32 it retains the main theme, and the WHDLoad patch supplies full joypad controls.

amiga:colonization.rationale: The ECS and AGA packages contain byte-identical game and German-language data, and contemporary catalog notes confirm that no graphically enhanced AGA edition was released. Prefer the lower-requirement ECS package.

amiga:defenderofthecrown.rationale: The CDTV edition preserves the original game while adding narration and a higher-fidelity medieval soundtrack.

amiga:deluxegalaga.rationale: The AGA edition is the most complete Deluxe Galaga release and improves presentation without changing the core game.

amiga:deluxepacman.rationale: The author's final full AGA release includes 128-color graphics, 50 levels, 15 bonus levels, and extra graphics and improvements; the ECS build uses 32-color graphics.

amiga:fightinspirit.rationale: AGA and CD32 share the enhanced 256-color presentation, while the extracted AGA package also contains the per-stage sound files absent from the CD32 package.

amiga:fireandice.rationale: The CD32 edition adds 256-color detailed parallax backgrounds, a different finale, extra music, and two-button jump support. The extracted WHDLoad package falls back to the local chip music but retains the visual and controller enhancements.

amiga:finalfight.rationale: The current package supersedes the separate 1 MB package, includes the enhanced 1 MB slave, and has newer fixes.

amiga:furyofthefurries.rationale: The CD32 edition adds AGA intro and inter-level graphics, more death animations, language selection, and direct save support.

amiga:gods.rationale: The current package removed the separate 1 MB chip-memory slave as unnecessary and contains newer fixes.

amiga:gunship2000.rationale: The CD32 release adds enhanced graphics, introduction, and sound, and the WHDLoad installer provides a distinct supported CD32 slave.

amiga:guardian.rationale: Guardian was developed for CD32 first and the computer release was stripped down. The extracted CD32 package retains the larger graphics set, full joypad controls, and NVRAM high-score saving.

amiga:internationalkarateplus.rationale: The CD release is otherwise a straight port but maps the keyboard-only trouser-drop action to the joypad, making it the better controller-first Batocera version.

amiga:intact.rationale: The 1.5 MB package uses the full-preload slave with all extra music; the lower-memory package only partially preloads game data.

amiga:junglestrike.rationale: The CD32 package contains materially higher-detail graphics, sprites, maps, intro, and music data than the floppy editions while retaining second-button weapon switching through the WHDLoad patch.

amiga:mortalkombat2.rationale: The current package replaces the older separate memory builds, supports both 512 KB and 1 MB chip-memory configurations, and includes newer fixes.

amiga:pinballillusions.rationale: AGA and CD32 use the same WHDLoad slave, but the CD32 game expects CD audio that is absent from the extracted PiMIGA package; AGA retains local in-game music data.

amiga:sabreteam.rationale: The CD32 release is based on the enhanced 256-color AGA edition and its extracted package adds title animations, speech samples, and substantially larger spoken briefing data.

amiga:simcity.rationale: The CDTV edition has a television-friendly closer city view, revised menu system, improved graphics, music data, and built-in German text; the WHDLoad patch supports hard-drive saves.

amiga:slamtilt.rationale: The standard package is the normal fast-memory configuration; the 2 MB slave is a constrained fallback for machines with chip memory only.

amiga:sleepwalker.rationale: The four-disk AGA edition enhances the in-game graphics and full-motion sequences over the three-disk ECS release.

amiga:shadowfighter.rationale: The CD32 release contains the same AGA disk images but its CD audio is disabled in the extracted WHDLoad package. The AGA installer adds fixes and hard-drive high-score saving without losing usable audio.

amiga:soccerkid.rationale: The extracted CD32 package includes multilingual data, additional cutscene graphics and music modules, while the AGA disk edition has a documented absence of title and in-game music.

amiga:speedball2.rationale: The CD32 edition preserves the original game while making opposing players fully distinct in color and providing controller-oriented input; its extracted package includes the revised music data.

amiga:superfrog.rationale: The CD32 release preserves the full game and intro while adding controller-oriented two-button support; the current WHDLoad patch also saves high scores.

amiga:superskidmarks.rationale: The CD32 package is the complete supported edition, includes assets missing from the floppy release, and enables the full high-memory multi-car configuration.

amiga:turrican.rationale: The CDTV release is the same game without enhancements and cannot save high scores; prefer the writable OCS WHDLoad release.

amiga:turrican2thefinalfight.rationale: The CDTV release has no gameplay enhancement and cannot save high scores; prefer the writable OCS WHDLoad release.

amiga:ufoenemyunknown.rationale: The AGA release has improved graphics and stronger review averages than OCS/ECS or CD32, while CD32 has a restrictive save limitation.

amiga:uridium2.rationale: The 2 MB chip-memory build enables the extended samples; the 1 MB build is the compatibility option.

amiga:universe.rationale: The extracted CD32 package predates the 2024 fix for broken CD32 saves and contains no CD-audio payload. The OCS package retains save-disk data and the native mouse interface.

amiga:worms.rationale: The 2 MB CD32 package is a superset of the byte-identical core game, includes all English, French, and German voice samples plus CD startup/title assets, and the PiMIGA source retains the separate CD32 AVI animation package.
